semantic_sweep.review_note: Reviewed the complete chapter, all sixteen exact source mappings, four proof targets, twenty-one Lean declarations, release and change-packet fixtures, reader bridge, every manifest atom, and every prose candidate. Made the Evidence-Preserving Publication Transaction the distinct owner; expanded 18/12/18/18; preserved repository hygiene without chapter-core effect; and froze the natural independent multi-maintainer matched-workflow reproduction and transfer campaign.

scope.population: Exact books, editions, changes, sources, claims, proofs, tests, renders, audiences, derivatives, releases, rights, reviewers, consumers, environments, and times.
scope.environment: Sixteen mappings; release-record fixture; three valid/six invalid change packets; blocked reader candidate plus three valid and five invalid routes; synchronized publication surfaces; four targets; twenty-one finite declarations. No manuscript-quality, accessibility-approval, reader-value, external-reproduction, or transfer result.
scope.model: Evidence-Preserving Publication Transaction over canonical source, provenance, lane separation, claim and proof scope, change packets, regeneration, validation, typed derivatives, release identity, correction, successor continuity, and expiry.
scope.authority: Owns publication-state integrity only; author intent, source truth, claim evidence, editorial quality, accessibility approval, rights, release approval, external publication, and reader judgment remain separate.
scope.time: Reviewed through 2026-07-15; material source, claim, proof, validation, rights, audience, format, release policy, consumer, environment, or time changes expire affected transactions.
scope.artifact: Chapter, manifest, sixteen mappings, release/change/reader fixtures and validators, publication surfaces, four targets, twenty-one declarations, semantic review, and future campaign.

assumptions: Canonical source, changes, claims, proofs, tests, renders, derivatives, releases, rights, reviews, corrections, and consumers can be identified and versioned.
Automated checks and human editorial, accessibility, rights, and publication judgments can remain distinct.
Matched publication workflows can receive equal content, tools, time, and review opportunity.
Independent maintainers, editors, accessibility reviewers, readers, repositories, institutions, formats, hosts, and domains can reproduce outcomes.
chapter_defaults.strongest_counterclaim: The workflow is elaborate repository bureaucracy: ordinary scholarly editing, Git, docs-as-code, or executable books can achieve equal accuracy and readability with less maintenance burden and fewer stale generated surfaces.
chapter_defaults.falsifier: Matched natural update streams show no improvement in claim accuracy, traceability, correction, reproducibility, rollback, accessibility, reader comprehension, or continuity; excessive burden and drift; or no independent reproduction and transfer.

required_lanes.necessity: Position the method against publication, governance, evaluation, and maintenance literature without inheriting results.
Justify author, editorial, accessibility, rights, release, correction, and publication authority.
Specify finite record and derivative boundaries without claiming scholarship or reader outcomes.
Exercise natural changes, generation, validation, derivatives, releases, corrections, rollback, and handoffs.
Measure accuracy, calibration, traceability, correction, reproducibility, accessibility, comprehension, quality, burden, resources, and cost.
Ablate source notes, claim records, proof review, validators, derivative boundaries, release identity, correction, and successor continuity.
Reproduce across maintainers, tools, repositories, institutions, formats, hosts, domains, and time.

contrary_evidence: Repository hygiene and green builds do not establish semantic, editorial, accessibility, or reader quality.
The change packets and reader bridge are synthetic or hand-authored finite records.
The blocked reader candidate has local format evidence but still lacks exact approval states.
All twenty-one declarations reduce hand-authored finite predicates, records, or routes.
External comparators are not external review or certification.
known_confounds: Maintainer expertise, content difficulty, tool familiarity, review effort, incentives, and existing repository quality can dominate outcomes.
Outcome-aware validators, samples, baselines, stopping, errors, and selected edits can bias the campaign.
Shared authors, models, tools, evaluators, organizations, or infrastructure can create false independence.
A complete publication record can encode false sources, claims, proofs, reviews, approvals, rights, or public state.
acceptance_criteria: Bind exact source commit, change, sources, claims, proofs, tests, audiences, derivatives, rights, reviews, release, correction, consumers, environment, time, and non-claims.
Run natural update streams against matched publishing baselines with complete errors, failures, reversions, costs, and reader outcomes.
Demonstrate correction, revocation, rollback, derivative repair, release identity, and successor continuity across all consumers.
Promotion survives causal ablation, independent reproduction, and heterogeneous transfer.
narrowing_criteria: Narrow every result to exact book, content, maintainer, tool, model, reviewer, audience, format, institution, host, domain, environment, and time.
Separate local build, render, inspection, accessibility, editorial approval, release approval, deployment, observation, archive, and publication.
Unknown semantic quality, rights, accessibility, comprehension, causal benefit, burden, reproduction, or transfer remains blocked.
refutation_criteria: Refute transactions with canonical drift, fabricated or mis-scoped records, invalid rights, stale projections, missing corrections, derivative mismatch, release identity failure, or lost successor state.
Refute benefit when matched workflows perform equally or added bureaucracy, selection, weak attacks, hidden review, or omitted failures explains gains.
Refute scholarship, chapter truth, reader value, governance, transfer, AGI, ASI, or SOTA from repositories, validators, proofs, renders, or releases.
deprecation_criteria: Deprecate only after every source, claim, proof, test, derivative, release, correction, residual, consumer, and successor state migrates with immutable lineage.

reproduction.command: python3 scripts/validate_living_book_change_packets.py && python3 scripts/validate_reader_release_candidate_bridge.py && python3 scripts/validate_outline_consistency.py && python3 scripts/validate_claim_atom_registry.py && cd lean && lake build AsiStackProofs.LivingBook
reproduction.environment_lock: Repository-local publication fixtures, validators, and module current through 2026-07-15; natural matched workflow campaign not run.
reproduction.evaluator_identity: Repository-local; independent maintainers, editors, accessibility reviewers, readers, repositories, institutions, formats, hosts, and domains required.

residual.next_unblocking_condition: Run a prospectively frozen natural update stream across independent human and AI maintainers against matched publishing workflows with injected errors, reader/accessibility/editorial outcomes, complete burden and cost, reproduction, and transfer.

non_claims: No source count, claim matrix, theorem, validator, render, format file, release, site, or polished prose establishes chapter truth, editorial quality, accessibility, reader value, capability, safety, AGI, ASI, or SOTA.
No natural matched multi-maintainer campaign, independent reader/accessibility/editorial evaluation, external reproduction, or transfer exists.
The argument-exit campaign is future work, not a result.

living-book-methodology.core.strongest_counterclaim: Ordinary scholarly and docs-as-code workflows match the benefits with less burden.
living-book-methodology.core.falsifier: Natural matched update streams show no quality, traceability, correction, reproduction, accessibility, reader, or continuity benefit, unacceptable burden, or no transfer.

prose_review_lineage.retired_sentence: They do not prove source interpretation, editorial quality, accessibility approval, chapter truth, reader or audio approval, external reproduction, transfer, or a chapter-core support effect.

preserved_disposition.rationale: Semantically reviewed as bounded explanation, source report, publication-lifecycle restatement, finite-proof boundary, scanner fragment, or explicit non-claim; material obligations are already atomized.

prose_review_lineage.retirement_reason: The sentence was rewritten to remove formulaic prose while preserving its bounded meaning; the old sentence hash is no longer current prose.

non_claims: The chapter core remains argument and its support-state effect is none.
Repository release discipline is not manuscript, accessibility, or reader-quality proof.
Formal declarations enforce finite record boundaries and do not prove scholarship outcomes.
External comparators are lineage and do not certify the method.
